target.name: XMOS
target.founder: Ali Dixon, James Foster, Noel Hurley, David May, Hitesh Mehta

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
XMOS is a growth-stage semiconductor company that has raised over £200 million to date. The Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 14 same-sector peers, including 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers such as Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Standard triangulation methods face material constraints here. Peer-median valuation is not applicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints. A broad all-sector median would obscure stage and business model differences. Public-comp anchoring is also not applicable because XMOS, as a private company, does not disclose revenue. The public Semiconductors universe trades at a median 20.6x price-to-sales across the three tickers identified, but this multiple cannot be applied without the target's top-line figures.

The analysis therefore cannot produce a quantitative valuation range at this time. Resolution requires either disclosure of XMOS's revenue or assembly of sufficient same-stage private comparables with disclosed valuations and financial metrics. The key uncertainties are the company's current revenue run-rate and the precedent valuations and multiples among its closest peer set.
